Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 3, 059 [SB Gilgameš 05][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
o i 1o i 1

dḫum-ba-⸢ba⸣ pa-a-šu₂ DU₃-ma DU₁₁.GA MUra a-na dGIŠ-gim₂-⸢maš⸣

Humbaba opened his mouth to speak, saying to Gilgameš:

o i 22

lim-tal-ku lil-lu dGIŠ-gim₂-maš nu-uʾ-u₂ a-me-lu mi-na-a ⸢tal-li⸣-[ka]  ; :. a-di ⸢IGI-ia⸣

'Let idiots, Gilgameš, consult a very stupid man. Why did you come into my presence?

o i 33

al-ka den-ki-du₃ DUMU KU₆ ša₂ la i-du-u₂ AD-šu₂

'Come, Enkidu, spawn of a fish, who did not know his father,

o i 44

⸢a⸣-tam!(NA) raq-qu u₃ NIG₂.BUN₂.NAku₆ ša₂ la i-ni-qu ši-zib AMA-šu₂

'hatchling of a raqqu-turtle and a šeleppû-turtle, who did not suck his mother's milk!

o i 55

⸢i⸣-na ṣe-ḫe-ri-ka a-dag-gal-ka-ma ul a-qer-ru-bu-ka

'In your youth I would look at you but I would not approach you,

o i 66

[x (x)] ⸢ta?⸣-da-ku-ka-a ul-tab-ba-a ina kar-ši-ia

'[...] ... in my belly.1

o i 77

[... lem]-⸢niš⸣ dGIŠ-gim₂-maš tu-šak-ši-du a-di maḫ-ri-ia

'[...] did you bring Gilgameš into my presence with evil intent,

o i 88

u ⸢at⸣-[ta ki]-⸢i⸣ lu₂KUR₂ a-ḫi-i ta!(GA-)az-zi-zu

'and (why) did you yourself stand like a foreign enemy?

o i 99

lu-⸢uk⸣-[kis x d]GIŠ-gim₂-maš nap-ša₂-ri u ki-ša₂-du

'Let me cut the throat and neck [...] Gilgameš,

o i 1010

lu-ša₂-⸢kil UZU⸣-ME-šu₂ ⸢iṣ-ṣur⸣ ṣar-ṣa-ri na-ʾi-ri a-re-e u zi-i-bi

'let me feed his flesh to cricket birds, screecher eagles, and vultures.'

o i 1111

dGIŠ-⸢gim₂-maš⸣ pa-a-šu₂ DU₃-⸢ma⸣ DU₁₁.GA MUra ana den-ki-du₃

Gilgameš opened his mouth to speak, saying to Enkidu:

o i 1212

ib-ri [x d]⸢ḫum⸣-ba-ba ⸢iš-ta⸣-nu-u₂ pa-ni-šu₂

'My friend, Humbaba's face has changed.

o i 1313

<<u>> gaš-⸢ri-iš ni⸣-te-la-a ana ⸢KI?-šu₂⸣ ni-kaš-šad-⸢su?⸣ ša₂-a-šu₂

'Mightily we have come up to his place to defeat him himself,

o i 1414

u₃ lib₃-bi ⸢ip-la⸣-ḫu ul i-⸢pa⸣-[ša₂]-⸢ḫu a⸣-[di] sur-riš

'but the heart that feared does not quickly calm down.'

o i 1515

den-ki-du₃ pa-a-šu₂ DU₃-ma ⸢DU₁₁⸣.[GA] MUra [x dGIŠ]-⸢gim₂⸣-maš

Enkidu opened his mouth to speak, saying [...] Gilgameš:

o i 1616

am-mi-ni ib-ri pi-is-nu-qiš!(UG) [ta]-⸢qab⸣-bi

'Why, my friend, do you speak like a weakling?

o i 1717

u₃ pi-i-ka ir-ma-am-ma tu-⸢lam⸣-[man] ⸢lib₃⸣-bi

'And your mouth slackened, troubling my heart.

o i 1818

e-nen-na-ma ib-ri iš-ta-at [(x)] ⸢PI?⸣ [x]

'Now, my friend, one [...] ... [...].

o i 1919

ina ra-a-ṭu lu₂SIMUG e-ra-<a> ša₂-ba-ša₂-⸢a⸣

'Gathering up the copper from the channel-mould of the copper-founder?

o i 2020

tu-u₂-ru ana 1 DANNA-AM₃ na-pa-ḫu na-pi-iḫ-tu ana 1 DANNA-AM₃ x-⸢lu-u₂⸣

'Blowing on the coals for 1 double-hour, ... what is kindled for 1 double-hour?

o i 2121

ša₂-par a-bu-bu iš-tuḫ-ḫu la-pa-tu

'To send a flood is to lash with the whip.

o i 2222

[x] ⸢ta⸣-as-suḫ GIR₃-MIN-ka e ta-tu-ur ana ar₂-ki-ka

'You should [...] lift your feet, you should not turn back.

o i 2323

[...] x x x mi-ḫi-iṣ-ka du-un-nin

'[...] ... make your blow strong!'

o i 2424

[...] x x x [x x] x x x

(rest of column missing)
Column ii
o ii 1o ii 1

[...] x lu ṭar-du

(Humbaba saying:) '[...] ... may they (Gilgameš and Enkidu) be driven off.'

o ii 22

⸢iš-mi?-šu₂-nu⸣-ti-ma [x x] ru-u₂-qu

He heard them [...] a distance,

o ii 33

im-ḫaṣ qaq-qa-⸢ram⸣-ma PI x x uš-tam-ḫir-šu₂

he smote the very ground, ... he confronted him (Gilgameš).

o ii 44

ina a-si-du še-pi-šu₂-nu qaq-qa-ri i-bi-iš-šu₂

At the heels of their feet the ground was splitting,

o ii 55

ina sa-a-ri-šu₂-nu uḫ-tapte-pu-u₂ si-ra-ra u la-ba-na-nu

as they circled, Sirara and Lebanon were sundered.2

o ii 66

iṣ-ṣa-lim ur-pa-tu₄ pe-ṣi-tu₄

White cloud was turned black,

o ii 77

mu-tu₄ ki-ma im-ba-ri i-za-an-nun UGU-šu₂-un

death raining down on them like fog.

o ii 88

dUTU a-na dḫum-ba-ba id-kaš-šum-ma me-ḫe-e ra-bu-tu

Šamaš raised against Humbaba the great storm-winds:

o ii 99

tu₁₅U₁₈.LU tu₁₅SI.SA₂ tu₁₅KUR.RA tu₁₅MAR.TU tu₁₅ziq-qa

south wind, north wind, east wind, west wind, blast,

o ii 1010

tu₁₅ziq-qa-ziq-qa tu₁₅ša₂-par-ziq-qa im-ḫul-lu tu₁₅si-mur-ra

counterblast, gale, destructive wind, typhoon,

o ii 1111

a-sak-ku šu-ru-up-pu-u₂ me-ḫu-u₂ a-šam-šu-tu

demon wind, icy blast, storm wind, dust storm.

o ii 1212

13 TU₁₅-MEŠ it-bu-nim-ma ša₂ dḫum-ba-ba i-ṭu-u₂ pa-ni-šu₂

The 13 winds arose and Humbaba's face darkened -

o ii 1313

ul i-nak-kip a-na pa-ni-šu₂ ul i-ra-aḫ-ḫi-iṣ ana EGIR-šu₂

he cannot push forwards, he cannot kick backwards -

o ii 1414

u₃ gišTUKUL-MEŠ ša₂ dGIŠ-gim₂-maš ik-šu-du dḫum-ba-ba

and the weapons of Gilgameš conquered Humbaba.

o ii 1515

dḫum-ba-ba nap!(AB-)ša₂-tuš i-še-ʾe-e MUra ana dGIŠ-gim₂-maš

Humbaba sought his life, saying to Gilgameš:

o ii 1616

ṣe-eḫ-re-⸢e⸣-ti ⸢dGIŠ-gim₂-maš⸣ AMA-ka u₂-lid-ka

'You are young, Gilgameš, (as when) your mother gave birth to you,

o ii 1717

u i-lit-ti ša₂ ⸢f⸣[ri-mat—dNIN.SUN₂] at-ta

'but you are the offspring of Rimat-Ninsun.

o ii 1818

ina pi-i dUTU u KURi x x x-ma1

'By the command of Šamaš also the mountains ...,

o ii 1919

⸢pe⸣-er-ʾu-um-ma ša₂ ŠA₃ UNUGki LUGAL dGIŠ-⸢gim₂-maš⸣

'a very shoot from Uruk's midst is the king Gilgameš.

o ii 2020

[x] x x dGIŠ-gim₂-maš mi-i-⸢ti ul⸣ x-tar-⸢ri⸣ [(x)] x LU

'[...] ..., O Gilgameš, a dead man ... not ... [...] ...,

o ii 2121

[x x bal]-⸢ṭu⸣ ana be-li₂-⸢šu₂⸣ [...]

'[...] a survivor for his lord [...].

o ii 2222

dGIŠ-gim₂-maš ⸢e-ṭir ZIti₃⸣ [...]

'O Gilgameš, spare my life [...],

o ii 2323

lu-ši-ba-ak-kum₂-ma ina [...]

'may I dwell for you in [...].

o ii 2424

iṣ-ṣi ma-la taq-qa-ba-⸢a⸣ [...]

'Trees, as many as you command me, [...],

o ii 2525

lu-uṣ-ṣur-ka giššimGIR₂ ⸢giš?⸣[...]

'let me guard for you the myrtle, the [...],

o ii 2626

iṣ-ṣi bal-ti E₂.⸢GAL⸣ [...]

'trees, the pride of a palace [...].'

o ii 2727

[d]⸢en⸣-ki-du₃ pa-a-šu₂ DU₃-ma DU₁₁.GA ⸢MU⸣[ra ...]

Enkidu opened his mouth to speak, saying [...]:

o ii 2828

[ib]-⸢ri⸣ e taš-me ša₂ dḫum-ba-ba ⸢DU₁₁⸣.GA-[šu₂]

'My friend (Gilgameš), do not listen to what Humbaba says,

o ii 2929

[...] ⸢un⸣-nin-ni-šu₂ x [x] x x [...]

[...] his supplications ... [...] ... [...]'

o ii 3030

[...]-⸢lum⸣-ma x [...]

o ii 3131

[...] x x [...]

o ii 3232

[...] x [...]

(rest of column missing)
Column iii
o iii 1o iii 1

am-ra-ta-ma ṭe₃-e-me gišTIR-ia ṭe₃-e-me ⸢KU⸣ x [(...)]

(Humbaba saying:) 'You (Enkidu) are experienced in the nature of my forest, the nature ... [(...)],

o iii 22

u₃ ti-de-e ka-li-šu₂-nu ša₂ qa-be₂-e

'and you know all the (right) things to say.

o iii 33

lu-u₂ aš₂-ši-ka lu-u₂ a-lul-ka ina ne₂-reb  ; pa-pal-la gišTIR-ia₂

'If only I had picked you up (and) hanged you from a sapling at the entrance to my forest,3

o iii 44

lu-u₂ u₂-ša₂-kil UZU-ka iṣ-ṣur ṣar-ṣa-ri na-ʾi-i-ri  ; a-re-e u zi-i-⸢bi⸣

'if only I had fed your flesh to cricket birds, screecher eagles, and vultures!

o iii 55

e-nen-na-a-ma den-ki-du₃ KI-ka ša₂-⸢ki⸣-in ru-um-⸢mu⸣-[ʾa-a]

'Now, Enkidu, my release lies with you:

o iii 66

qi₂-bi-ma a-na dGIŠ-gim₂-maš ZI li-ṭi-ir

'speak to Gilgameš, so that he may spare my life!'

o iii 77

den-ki-du₃ pa-a-šu₂ DU₃-ma DU₁₁.GA MUra ana dGIŠ-⸢gim₂⸣-[maš]

Enkidu opened his mouth to speak, saying to Gilgameš:

o iii 88

ib-ri dḫum-ba-ba ma-aṣ-ṣar gišTIR giš[EREN]

'My friend, Humbaba, guardian of the forest of cedar,

o iii 99

gum₂-mir-šu₂ ne₂-er-šu ṭe₃-en-šu₂ ḫul-⸢liq⸣

'finish him off, kill him, destroy his nature!

o iii 1010

dḫum-ba-ba ma-aṣ-ṣar gišTIR gum₂-⸢mir⸣-[šu₂]  ; ne₂-er-šu ṭe₃-en-šu₂ ḫul-[liq]

'Humbaba, guardian of the forest, finish him off, kill him, destroy his nature,

o iii 1111

la-am iš-mu-u₂ a-ša₂-re-du d⸢EN⸣.[LIL₂]

'before the pre-eminent one, Ellil, hears!

o iii 1212

lib-ba-ti-ni i-mal₂-lu-u₂ DINGIR-⸢MEŠ⸣ [x x]

'The [...] gods could become full with rage against us,

o iii 1313

dEN.LIL₂ ina NIBRUki dUTU ina [...]

'Ellil in Nippur, Šamaš in [...].

o iii 1414

šu-uz-ziz-ma da-ra-a [...]

'Set up an eternal [...],

o iii 1515

ki-i dGIŠ-gim₂-maš dḫum-⸢ba⸣-[ba ...]

'how Gilgameš [...] Humbaba [...].'

o iii 1616

⸢iš⸣-me-e-ma dḫum-ba-ba [...]

Humbaba heard [...],

o iii 1717

[iš-ši]-⸢ma re-ši-šu₂ dḫum-ba⸣-[ba ...]

Humbaba raised his head and [...]

(rest of column missing)
Reverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
r i 1'r 1'

⸢e⸣-x [...]

r i 2'2'

e-ka-a-⸢ma⸣ [...]

(Enkidu saying:) 'Where [...]?'

r i 3'3'

iš-me-⸢e-ma⸣ [...]

[...] he (Humbaba) heard [...],

r i 4'4'

⸢iš-ši⸣-[ma ...]

[...] he raised [...] and [...] (saying to Enkidu:)

r i 5'5'

[...]

r i 6'6'

x [...] x x [...]

r i 7'7'

x [...] x-ma ma-li-⸢ku⸣ [...]

'... [...] ... adviser [...],

r i 8'8'

u₃ aš₂-bu ša₂ E₂-šu₂ gi-ra-a-tu₄ [...]

'and the resident in his house [...] hostilities.

r i 9'9'

aš₂-ba-ta ki-ma SIPA ma-⸢ḫar⸣-[šu₂]

'You are sitting like a shepherd before him,

r i 10'10'

u₃ ki-i a-gi-ir pi-i-šu₂ ⸢ta⸣-[...]

'and like a hireling under his command you [...].

r i 11'11'

e-nen-na-a-ma den-ki-du₃ KI-ka ša₂-kin [...]  ; u [...]

'Now, Enkidu, [...] lies with you, and [...]:

r i 12'12'

qi₂-bi-ma ana dGIŠ-gim₂-maš ZIti₃ ⸢li⸣-[ṭi-ir]

'speak to Gilgameš, so that he may spare my life!'

r i 13'13'

den-ki-du₃ pa-a-šu₂ DU₃-ma DU₁₁.GA MUra [...]

Enkidu opened his mouth to speak, saying [...]:

r i 14'14'

⸢ib⸣-ri dḫum-ba-⸢ba⸣ ma-aṣ-ṣar giš⸢TIR⸣ [...]  ; ne₂-er-šu-ma ⸢ṭe₃⸣-[en-šu₂ x x]

'My friend, Humbaba, guardian of the forest, [...], kill him, and [...] his nature,

r i 15'15'

⸢la-am⸣ iš-mu-u₂ a-ša₂-re-du [...]

'before the pre-eminent one, [...], hears!

r i 16'16'

⸢lib⸣-ba-ti-ni i-mal₂-lu-u₂ DINGIR-⸢MEŠ GAL?⸣-[MEŠ?]

'The great gods could become full with rage against us,

r i 17'17'

⸢dEN⸣.LIL₂ ina ⸢NIBRUki⸣ dUTU ina ⸢LARSAki⸣ [...]  ; [šu-uz-ziz]-⸢ma da-ra-a⸣ a-[...]

'Ellil in Nippur, Šamaš in Larsa [...]. Set up an eternal ... [...],

r i 18'18'

⸢ki-i⸣ dGIŠ-gim₂-maš <<DIŠ>> d⸢ḫum-ba-ba i-nar-ru?⸣ [...]

'how Gilgameš kills Humbaba [...].'

r i 19'19'

iš-me-e-ma d⸢ḫum-ba⸣-[ba] x x x [...]  ; u i-x [x] x [...]

Humbaba heard ... [...] and ... [...] ... [...]

Column ii
(start of column missing)
r ii 1'r 1'

a-a ⸢u₂⸣-[...]  ; [...]

(Humbaba saying:) 'May they not [...].

r ii 2'2'

a-a u₂-lab-bi-ra ki-lal-la-⸢an⸣

'May they both not grow old,

r ii 3'3'

UGU ib-ri-šu₂ dGIŠ-gim₂-maš den-ki-du₃ qe₂-bi!(IB-)ri  ; a-a ir-šu₂

'in addition to his friend Gilgameš, may Enkidu have nobody to bury him.'

r ii 4'4'

den-ki-du₃ pa-a-šu₂ DU₃-ma DU₁₁.GA MUra ana dGIŠ-gim₂-maš

Enkidu opened his mouth to speak, saying to Gilgameš:

r ii 5'5'

ib-ri a-qab-bi-kum₂-ma ul ta-šem-ma-an-ni

'My friend, I speak to you but you do not listen to me.

r ii 6'6'

a-⸢di ar-rat⸣ x [x x] x [(x)] x [x (x)]

'Until the curses ... [...] ... [(...)] ... [...],

r ii 7'7'

x [...] ⸢a-na pi-i⸣-šu₂

'... [...] to his mouth.'

r ii 8'8'

[... zi]-⸢kir⸣ ib-ri-šu₂

[...] the utterance of his friend,

r ii 9'9'

⸢iš-lu-up⸣ [nam-ṣa]-⸢ra⸣ [x x] i-di-šu₂

he drew the sword [...] his side.

r ii 10'10'

dGIŠ-gim₂-maš [x x] ⸢ki-ša₂⸣-dam-ma

Gilgameš [...] in the neck,

r ii 11'11'

<<x>> d⸢en⸣-[ki-du₃ ...]-BU-tu a-di ḫa-še-e iš-tal-pu

Enkidu [...] ... until he pulled out the lungs.

r ii 12'12'

[...] ⸢LA?⸣-nu i-šaḫ-ḫi-iṭ

[...] ... he jumps up,

r ii 13'13'

[ul]-⸢tu⸣ SAG.DU i-šul-lal šin-nu

from the head he carries off the ivory as booty.

r ii 14'14'

[...] ṭuḫ-du ana KURi im-qut

[...] plenty fell on the mountain,

r ii 15'15'

[...] x ṭuḫ-du ana KURi im-qut

[...] ... plenty fell on the mountain.

r ii 16'16'

[...] x x LA x x x x x x

r ii 17'17'

[...] x ⸢UŠ⸣ x x x x

(5 lines missing)
r ii 23'23'

[...] UZ ZA x x (x)-⸢a⸣-ti

Column iii
(start of column missing)
r iii 1'r 1'

[...]-⸢du-u₂ ib-ta-at⸣;qu-ni

[...] ... they have cut off,

r iii 2'2'

[x x] ⸢BI 6?⸣ ŠU.SI ti-rik sun-gin₃-ni-šu-nu

[...] ... 6 fingers was the blow of their wood shavings.

r iii 3'3'

⸢d⸣GIŠ-gim₂-maš iṣ-ṣi i-nak-ki-is  ; den-ki-du₃ iš-te-ne₂-ʾa-a ḫur-ba-zal-lu

Gilgameš was cutting down trees, Enkidu was constantly looking for the best timber.

r iii 4'4'

den-ki-du₃ pa-a-šu₂ DU₃-ma DU₁₁.GA MUra ana dGIŠ-gim₂-maš

Enkidu opened his mouth to speak, saying to Gilgameš:

r iii 5'5'

ib-ri nit-ta-ki-is gišEREN ši-i-ḫu

'My friend, we have cut down a tall cedar,

r iii 6'6'

ša₂ muḫ-ḫa-šu ANe nak-pi₂

'whose top abutted the heavens.

r iii 7'7'

e-pu-uš-ma gišIG ša₂ 6 NINDA me-lu-ša₂ 2 NINDA ru-pu;us-su

'I made a door, whose height is 6 rods, whose width is 2 rods,

r iii 8'8'

1 KUŠ₃ u₂-bu-šu₂ šu!(SU-)ku-ša₂ sa-ḫir-ša₂ u ša₂-gam-ma-ša₂  ; ša₂ iš-te-en-⸢ma⸣

'whose thickness is 1 cubit, whose doorpost, bottom pivot, and top pivot are from a single (piece).

r iii 9'9'

ana NIBRUki li-bil-lu id₂pu-⸢rat-tu₄⸣ [x x]  ; NIBRU⸢ki li⸣-[iḫ-du]

'Let the Euphrates carry (it) to Nippur, 'let the [...] of Nippur rejoice.'

r iii 10'10'

⸢ḫar⸣-mu am-mu la-⸢ru⸣-[u₂ ...]  ; [...]

... branches [...],

r iii 11'11'

ir-tak-su a-⸢mu⸣ [...]  ; [...]

they lashed together a raft, [...].

r iii 12'12'

den-ki-du₃ ra-kib [...]

Enkidu was aboard [...],

r iii 13'13'

u dGIŠ-⸢gim₂⸣-maš SAG.DU d⸢ḫum-ba-ba⸣ [...]

and Gilgameš [...] the head of Humbaba.

(catchline)
r iii 14'14'

im-su ma-le-[e-šu₂ ...]

He washed his dirty hair, [...].

(colophon)
r iii 15'15'

IM 5-KAM.MA ša₂ [...]

Tablet 5, 'He who [...', ...].

(rest of column missing)

1the sign U could also be read UMUN, giving bēl šadî, 'lord of the mountains'

1 or 'in my mind'

2 or 'they sundered Sirara and Lebanon'

3 literally 'at the entrance to a sapling of my forest'

Created by Frances Reynolds for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2008 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P348662
